deep_dive.title: GIFT: LLM-Guided State-Reward Interface for Financial Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Financial portfolio trading is naturally formulated as a reinforcement learning problem, where an agent sequentially rebalances assets under changing market conditions to balance return, risk, and transaction costs. Yet in non-stationary markets, raw OHLCV states and short-horizon return rewards often provide an under-specified learning interface, motivating large language models as a way to inject financial knowledge into state and reward design while constraining open-ended generation. To this end, we propose GIFT, an LLM-guided framework for state-reward interface design in PPO-based financial reinforcement learning. Rather than using the LLM to make trading decisions, GIFT uses Factor-guided State Enhancement to generate state features from financial-factor primitives, Risk-rule-guided Reward Shaping to generate auxiliary rewards from portfolio-risk rules, and Diagnostic-guided Refinement to revise candidate interfaces using PPO rollout diagnostics. After refinement, GIFT fixes the selected state-reward interface before evaluation, with no further LLM queries or interface updates at test time. Comprehensive rolling-window experiments across diverse market regimes and portfolio scenarios demonstrate that GIFT improves learning-signal quality and out-of-sample risk-adjusted portfolio performance over baselines. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/KAG778/GIFT .
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Uses LLM to design state-reward for PPO-based financial trading, directly an RL-based post-training technique for financial agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用大语言模型为基于PPO的金融交易设计状态与奖励，直接体现了金融智能体的强化学习后训练
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用大语言模型为基于PPO的金融交易设计状态与奖励，直接体现了金融智能体的强化学习后训练
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用大语言模型为基于PPO的金融交易设计状态与奖励，直接体现了金融智能体的强化学习后训练
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GIFT, an LLM-guided state-reward interface for financial RL, improving portfolio trading by injecting domain knowledge into PPO training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为解决金融投资组合交易中原始状态和短视奖励的信息不足问题，提出GIFT框架，利用大语言模型从金融因子中生成增强状态，并设计多维奖励，随后用PPO训练交易智能体，实验显示相比传统RL接口能取得更稳健的收益。该工作展示了LLM与RL结合在金融领域后训练的应用潜力。

deep_dive.title: Exploring Agentic Tool-Calling Decisions via Uncertainty-Aligned Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based agents often make suboptimal tool-use decisions, including unsupported tool invocation and hallucinated direct responses, which may accumulate errors throughout multi-step interactions. Existing approaches mainly improve these behaviors through inference-time correction or coarse-grained reward signals based on decision outcomes and structured checklists, leaving the uncertainty characteristics of agent decisions underexplored. We observe that decision-oriented reinforcement learning tends to weaken the uncertainty separation between correct and incorrect actions, resulting in overconfident mistakes and weaker exploration signals. Therefore, we propose TRUST, which incorporates uncertainty quantification into reward design as a repulsive force for maintaining uncertainty separation, and labels lightweight key-turn annotations for unified post-training of multi-turn trajectories. Experimental results across diverse tool-use benchmarks show that TRUST consistently enhances both decision quality and agent performance while maintaining more reliable uncertainty estimates during optimization.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes RL method to improve agent tool-use via uncertainty alignment, a post-training technique for autonomous agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出通过不确定性对齐的强化学习方法改进智能体工具使用决策，属于面向自主智能体的后训练技术
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出通过不确定性对齐的强化学习方法改进智能体工具使用决策，属于面向自主智能体的后训练技术
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出通过不确定性对齐的强化学习方法改进智能体工具使用决策，属于面向自主智能体的后训练技术
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses suboptimal tool-calling in LLM agents by incorporating uncertainty quantification into RL training, aligning decision confidence and improving robustness.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型智能体在工具调用中常出现决策次优、过度自信错误等问题，提出TRUST方法，将不确定性量化和决策对齐纳入强化学习训练框架，使智能体能更好地区分正确与错误动作，实验表明提升了多步交互中的决策鲁棒性。该方法为自主智能体的后训练提供了新的技术路线。

deep_dive.title: SkillHone: A Harness for Continual Agent Skill Evolution Through Persistent Decision History
deep_dive.abstract: Agent skills extend language-model agents with task-specific procedures, scripts, and references, but the tasks and environments they target continually change. Existing methods improve skills in bounded runs and retain only the final artifact, discarding the decision history that later agents need to interpret prior revisions, evaluations, and rejected alternatives. We introduce SkillHone, a harness for continual agent skill evolution grounded in persistent decision history. SkillHone pairs skill revisions with evaluation-side evidence that supplies practice feedback, recording structured histories of diagnoses, revisions, evidence, and outcomes. Role-separated subagents run candidate skills on practice probes with redacted reporting and propose revisions informed by prior decisions, enabling cross-session refinement without rediscovering past rationale. We evaluate SkillHone on deep-research benchmarks in a raw open-web setting, where agents are not given an integrated search stack and must organize retrieval through portable skills. We compare against a deep-research agent backed by commercial retrieval services. With Qwen3.6-35B-A3B as the evaluation-time backbone, the resulting skills outperform the deep-research agent by 15.8 points on GAIA and 3.2 points on WebWalkerQA-EN, while also exceeding prior skill-evolution methods.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: harness for continual agent skill evolution with persistent decision history
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过持久决策历史实现智能体技能持续演化的工具
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过持久决策历史实现智能体技能持续演化的工具
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过持久决策历史实现智能体技能持续演化的工具
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces SkillHone, a harness that improves agent skills continuously by recording full decision histories including revisions, evaluations, and rejected alternatives, using practice probes with redacted reports for skill refinement.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有方法在有限运行中改进智能体技能并仅保留最终产物而丢弃决策历史的问题，本文提出SkillHone工具，通过持久化记录诊断、修订、证据和结果的完整历史，结合红膜报告实践探针进行反馈，实现智能体技能的连续演化，为智能体后训练中的技能迭代提供了有效机制。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对现有方法在有限运行中改进智能体技能并仅保留最终产物而丢弃决策历史的问题，本文提出SkillHone工具，通过持久化记录诊断、修订、证据和结果的完整历史，结合红膜报告实践探针进行反馈，实现智能体技能的连续演化，为智能体后训练中的技能迭代提供了有效机制。

deep_dive.title: One Model, Multiple Goals: Adaptive Multi-Objective Learning for E-commerce Dialogue Systems
deep_dive.abstract: Dialogue systems in e-commerce scenarios often need to satisfy multiple objectives: accurately reasoning over user profiles (e.g., eligibility, credit limit) to ensure correct decision-making and user state interpretation, while also generating natural and faithful responses. These goals are complementary but not identical. In this work, we propose MORE, an adaptive Multi-Objective REinforcement learning framework that jointly optimizes reasoning accuracy and linguistic naturalness. Our preliminary experiments show that directly mixing rewards with diverging optimization dynamics can cause oscillations and unstable learning. Thus, instead of optimizing a single mixed reward, we treat reasoning functions as constraints that guide policy optimization. At inference time, the system directly generates responses without explicit reasoning steps, while still benefiting from reasoning-enhanced scaffold and avoiding additional inference overhead. To better balance linguistic objectives during response generation, we introduce an adaptive multi-reward mechanism that aggregates signals such as fluency and naturalness and dynamically reweighs them via gradient feedback. We evaluate MORE on two real-world dialogue systems at ByteDance and the MultiWOZ 2.2 benchmark, where it consistently outperforms strong baselines. In 14-day online experiments on ByteDance production traffic, MORE improves overall and reached conversion by 16.53% and 30.09%, while increasing user satisfaction and reducing handoff rates. Notably, in a human-machine comparison, MORE recovers about 60% of the incremental conversion lift achieved by human agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Applies multi-objective RL to optimize dialogue agents, a post-training technique for autonomous conversational agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 应用多目标强化学习联合优化对话智能体，属于自主对话智能体的后训练技术
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 应用多目标强化学习联合优化对话智能体，属于自主对话智能体的后训练技术
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 应用多目标强化学习联合优化对话智能体，属于自主对话智能体的后训练技术
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MORE, a multi-objective RL framework that balances reasoning accuracy and response naturalness in dialogue systems by treating reasoning as constraints.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对电商对话系统需同时满足推理准确与回复自然等多目标需求，提出MORE多目标强化学习框架，将推理功能作为约束引导策略优化，避免直接混合奖励导致的训练振荡。该范式为对话智能体的后训练提供了兼顾准确性与流利性的解决方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对电商对话系统需同时满足推理准确与回复自然等多目标需求，提出MORE多目标强化学习框架，将推理功能作为约束引导策略优化，避免直接混合奖励导致的训练振荡。该范式为对话智能体的后训练提供了兼顾准确性与流利性的解决方法。

quick_skim.title: Customer-Agent: Overcoming Context Limitations in Ultra-Long Shopping Trajectories via Tool-Augmented Agents and RLVR
quick_skim.abstract: Understanding customer shopping trajectories is essential for enabling personalized shopping experiences. However, shopping records (i.e., customer's search, clicks, purchases, etc.) often span long time horizons over multiple years, resulting in extremely long trajectories that pose significant challenges for existing large language models (LLMs). Despite the importance of this problem, existing benchmarks are limited to short customer trajectories, while real-world trajectories from large e-commerce platforms are rarely accessible due to data privacy constraints. To address this gap, we introduce ShopTrajQA, a long-context evaluation benchmark constructed from real-world product information and simulated shopping trajectories. The dataset includes variants of up to 32k and 64k tokens, enabling systematic evaluation of model robustness under varying context lengths. Through comprehensive benchmarking of frontier LLMs, we identify critical performance gaps in reasoning over long shopping trajectory data. To address these challenges, we propose a Customer Agent Framework for ultra-long context management. Leveraging a Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) agentic training paradigm, our approach stores trajectories as external local files and trains the agent to autonomously retrieve and parse them through code-interpreter interactions (e.g., SQL queries), effectively bypassing the fixed in-context window constraints of LLMs. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework achieves strong performance for ShopTrajQA and shows generalization to other complex reasoning tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RLVR training of tool-augmented agents for long-horizon shopping trajectories
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用RLVR训练工具增强智能体处理长周期购物轨迹
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用RLVR训练工具增强智能体处理长周期购物轨迹
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用RLVR训练工具增强智能体处理长周期购物轨迹
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ShopTrajQA benchmark and a Customer-Agent trained with RLVR to handle ultra-long shopping trajectories using tool-augmented LLMs, showing improved long-context understanding.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 电商购物轨迹横跨多年，给LLM的长上下文理解带来挑战。本文提出长上下文评估基准ShopTrajQA，并设计了一种工具增强智能体Customer-Agent，利用RLVR（基于向量奖励的强化学习）进行训练。实验表明，该智能体能够有效处理超长时序用户行为，在购物轨迹问答任务上性能优异，其RLVR训练方法为金融等长周期轨迹智能体的构建提供了可参考的后训练范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 电商购物轨迹横跨多年，给LLM的长上下文理解带来挑战。本文提出长上下文评估基准ShopTrajQA，并设计了一种工具增强智能体Customer-Agent，利用RLVR（基于向量奖励的强化学习）进行训练。实验表明，该智能体能够有效处理超长时序用户行为，在购物轨迹问答任务上性能优异，其RLVR训练方法为金融等长周期轨迹智能体的构建提供了可参考的后训练范式。

quick_skim.title: How Small Can You Go? LoRA Fine-Tuning 270M-8B Models for Merchant Information Extraction in Financial Transactions
quick_skim.abstract: Financial transaction processing requires extracting structured merchant information from noisy, abbreviated bank transaction strings at scale. Our current production system, a LoRA-fine-tuned LLaMA 3.1-8B, achieves 96.95% F1 on this task, but deploying 8-billion-parameter models imposes prohibitive memory, latency, and cost constraints. To identify more efficient alternatives, we conduct a deployment-focused study of 24 model variants spanning four model families: Gemma 3 (270M, 1B, 4B), Qwen 3.5 (0.8B, 2B, 4B), Aya (3.35B), and LLaMA 3.1-8B, systematically evaluating accuracy, inference throughput, training cost, and hardware behavior to assess production suitability. Our findings show that: (1) reproducing the LLaMA 3.1-8B fine-tune with a LoRA rank of 8 achieves 96.75% F1, only 0.20 points below the rank-32 baseline; (2) Qwen 3.5 4B with JSON-only prompting reaches 96.60% F1, within 0.35 points of the 8B baseline while using roughly half the parameters; (3) the 0.8B Qwen 3.5 model achieves 94.75% F1, matching models 2.5-4x larger and offering an attractive latency-accuracy trade-off; (4) chain-of-thought fine-tuning generally improves F1 by 0.3-1.8 points across most models, although Qwen 3.5 4B performs best with direct JSON-only prompting; and (5) Qwen 3.5 Think and Nothink training templates produce nearly identical results (F1 differences <0.004), indicating that explicit reasoning supervision is unnecessary for structured extraction tasks. We further deploy all 14 fine-tuned sub-8B models as Databricks Model Serving endpoints and observe that benchmark performance transfers reliably to production, with an average F1 change of only 0.8 points. Aya 3.35B, based on the Cohere2 architecture, is the sole exception, exhibiting a 3-5 point decline under serving conditions. Based on these results, we provide deployment recommendations across accuracy and latency requirements, ...
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LoRA fine-tuning of LLMs for financial transaction merchant extraction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用LoRA微调大型语言模型进行金融交易商户信息抽取
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用LoRA微调大型语言模型进行金融交易商户信息抽取
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用LoRA微调大型语言模型进行金融交易商户信息抽取
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies efficient LoRA fine-tuning from 270M to 8B models for financial merchant extraction, finding smaller models can match large ones with lower cost, enabling production deployment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 金融交易处理需从嘈杂银行流水字符串中提取结构化商户信息，但大模型部署成本高。本研究系统评估了24个模型变体（Gemma 3、Qwen 3.5等）经LoRA微调后的效果，发现较小模型（如2.7亿参数）在精度接近的同时，显著降低了推理延迟和成本，为金融领域大模型轻量化微调提供了参考。
